evidence.description: CRIT-001 requires the CLI's canonical parameter surface (enums/defaults/aliases/conflict rules) to match behavioral-spec.md §2 exactly, with an adversary spot-check of at least 6 commands as the passThreshold.

Section 2 (per-command specs, REQ-101..REQ-181) states the exact canonical parameter surface for all 18 commands, each with an inline src/tools/*.ts file:line citation back to the blockrun-mcp clone, satisfying CRIT-001's 'line-cited to the clone' requirement.
Section 6 (Traceability) records a positional-vs-canonical audit across all 18 commands confirming no REQ was silently left as an undocumented deviation.

CRIT-002 names the required edge-case classes: alias/positional conflicts, budget cap interactions (ephemeral vs persisted vs --max-quote-usd), path-safety, SSRF guards, 402-quote re-validation, payment-failure no-charge paths.

REQ-018/REQ-019/REQ-019a-c/REQ-020 specify the ephemeral per-invocation cap vs the persisted ~/.blockrun/cli-budget.json ledger vs the video-specific --max-quote-usd gate as three independent, additive checks.

Section 3 (path-traversal/input safety, REQ-200/201) and Section 5 (payment/budget cross-cutting, REQ-220/221) cover SSRF/path-safety guards and the mandatory re-validation of a real 402 quote against both caps before signing.
PROP-200/201/203 assign these edge cases concrete Tier-1/Tier-3 verification mechanisms (hasPathTraversal/isValidNetworkSlug unit tests; reReserveIfHigher unit + E2E quote-vs-estimate capture).
CRIT-003 requires full suite green (376/376), clean typecheck/build, and ported-logic fidelity, with a passThreshold requiring the adversary's own re-run.
target-feature-tests / new-feature-tests / regression-baseline all report PASS (376/376), matching the count and outcome CRIT-003 requires at contract time.
`npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit) and `npm run build` (tsup) both complete cleanly with no reported errors, satisfying CRIT-003's typecheck/build-clean clause.
Three consecutive full `npm test` runs (376/376 pass, 0 fail, 0 cancelled each time) demonstrate stability rather than a single lucky run, directly supporting the re-run-based passThreshold.

CRIT-004 requires blockrun-mcp conventions (ESM/.js imports, kebab-case, 1 command = 1 file, <800 lines, no new linter/formatter) and a pure-core/impure-shell purity boundary with zero network/fs in core.
Section 1.1 enumerates every pure-core module (src/core/*.ts) and its 1:1 port origin in the blockrun-mcp clone, with an explicit purity contract (no fetch/fs/process.env/Date.now/Math.random).
Section 1.2 fences all network/filesystem/wallet-key I/O to src/shell/*.ts and src/commands/*.ts, naming commands/*.ts as the only layer allowed to import an @blockrun/llm client class or call fetch.

Independent grep of every file under src/core/ for `from 'node:fs'`, `from 'node:net'`, `from 'undici'`, and `from '@blockrun` imports returned zero matches, confirming the pure core is import-clean as CRIT-004's passThreshold requires.
Directory listing of src/ (66 files) shows the args/commands/core/shell split matches the verification-architecture.md design table 1:1, with one file per command under both args/ and commands/.
CRIT-005 requires the Tier-3 E2E plan to be executable exactly as spec'd against the built dist/index.js, with each verification-architecture.md §4.3 row mapping to a runnable command.

Section 4 (Tier-3 E2E design): §4.1 hermetic HOME-sandbox isolation mechanism, §4.2 the $10 hard budget cap, §4.3 the full per-command minimum-cost real-path table (all 18 commands, each with an exact CLI invocation and expected cost) — every row names a concrete, runnable `blockrun <command> <flags>` invocation against the built binary.

§4.4 explicitly documents the sub-paths NOT exercised (realface enroll, phone lookup/buy/renew/voice-call, video at non-minimal tiers) with reasons recorded rather than silently skipped, and §4.5 defines the VERIFICATION.md evidence-ledger row shape (command/invocation/chain/wallet/HOME/tx-hash/cost/pass-fail/timestamp) required for every Tier-3 row including untested ones.
